mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all historical periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which compresses corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and durable goods demand, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 6-month trend signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, which forces firms to cut capital expenditures and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 70% of periods where INDPRO trades below its 6-month moving average during post-shock recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains above 3.0% while the federal funds rate stays below 5.0%, markets price in aggressive future rate hikes that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns show no significant negative drift when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 and FEDFUNDS remains below 5.0, with a t-statistic greater than -1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows no predictive power for 5-day S&P 500 returns, with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising retail sales indicates inventory depletion and supply chain strain, which raises input costs and squeezes margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines while RSAFS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of observations where INDPRO falls and RSAFS rises simultaneously during late-cycle phases.
